entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in the Ethiopian highlands

entities.name: WHEAT YIELD
entities.description: The amount of wheat produced per unit area, affected by landscape position and nutrients

entities.name: FERTILIZER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which crops utilize applied fertilizers

entities.name: CATENA
entities.description: A conceptual framework describing a sequence of soils that vary with landscape position

entities.name: LANDSCAPE POSITIONS
entities.description: Topographic locations such as hillslopes, midslopes, and footslopes

entities.name: HILLSLOPES
entities.description: Higher landscape positions characterized by lower soil organic carbon and water content

entities.name: MIDSLOPES
entities.description: Intermediate landscape positions within a catena

entities.name: FOOTSLOPES
entities.description: Lower landscape positions showing higher crop response to fertilizers

entities.name: EAST AFRICAN HIGHLANDS
entities.description: The broad geographic region where the study is situated

entities.name: ETHIOPIAN HIGHLANDS
entities.description: The specific region in East Africa where experiments were conducted

entities.name: ENDAMOHONI
entities.description: A wheat-growing district in the Ethiopian highlands

entities.name: LEMO
entities.description: A wheat-growing district in the Ethiopian highlands

entities.name: WORREILU
entities.description: A wheat-growing district in the Ethiopian highlands

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A primary nutrient used in fertilizers to improve crop growth

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A primary nutrient used in fertilizers to improve crop growth

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A nutrient whose application significantly benefits yield during dry years

entities.name: SULFUR
entities.description: A nutrient with limited yield benefit in the studied context

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: A micronutrient with limited yield benefit in the studied context

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: A soil component that decreases with increasing slope

entities.name: CLAY CONTENT
entities.description: A soil property that decreases with increasing slope

entities.name: SOIL WATER CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of water in the soil, which decreases with increasing slope

entities.name: CROP NITROGEN RECOVERY FRACTION
entities.description: A measure of the proportion of applied nitrogen taken up by the crop

entities.name: CROP WATER PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: A measure of crop yield per unit of water used

entities.name: ON-FARM REPLICATED EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The research method used to assess landscape positions and nutrient responses

entities.name: FERTILIZER RECOMMENDATIONS
entities.description: The process of determining optimal fertilizer application rates

entities.name: CONSERVATION MEASURES
entities.description: Practices used to prevent nutrient movement and soil erosion on hillslopes

entities.name: DRY YEARS
entities.description: Environmental conditions under which potassium application is most beneficial

entities.name: ORGANIC FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Fertilizers derived from organic sources, recommended for hillslopes

entities.name: MINERAL FERTILIZER
entities.description: Chemically synthesized fertilizers that may increase nutrient runoff on hillslopes

entities.name: CROP-NUTRIENT RESPONSES
entities.description: The reaction of a crop to the application of various nutrients

entities.name: RESOURCES USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a crop utilizes available resources like water and nutrients

entities.name: UNDULATING LANDSCAPES
entities.description: Topographic features characterized by uneven terrain in East Africa

entities.name: SOIL VARIABILITY
entities.description: The variation in soil properties across a landscape

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of the land to produce crops

relationships.description: WHEAT yield is the primary productivity measure for the crop
LANDSCAPE POSITIONS significantly affect WHEAT YIELD
CATENA is the conceptual framework for understanding LANDSCAPE POSITIONS
HILLSLOPES are a specific type of LANDSCAPE POSITIONS
MIDSLOPES are a specific type of LANDSCAPE POSITIONS
FOOTSLOPES are a specific type of LANDSCAPE POSITIONS
LANDSCAPE POSITIONS dictate the efficiency of fertilizer use
FOOTSLOPES show higher crop response to fertilizers than hillslopes
HILLSLOPES show lower crop response to fertilizers than footslopes
Increasing slope leads to a decrease in SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
Increasing slope leads to a decrease in CLAY CONTENT
Increasing slope leads to a decrease in SOIL WATER CONTENT
POTASSIUM application significantly benefits yield in DRY YEARS
POTASSIUM benefits are specifically observed during DRY YEARS
ZINC application provides hardly any yield benefit
SULFUR application provides hardly any yield benefit
CROP NITROGEN RECOVERY FRACTION decreases with increasing slope
CROP WATER PRODUCTIVITY decreases with increasing slope
HILLSLOPES are better served by ORGANIC FERTILIZERS
HILLSLOPES are better served by CONSERVATION MEASURES
High rates of MINERAL FERTILIZER on hillslopes increase nutrient movement risk
ENDAMOHONI is a district within the ETHIOPIAN HIGHLANDS
LEMO is a district within the ETHIOPIAN HIGHLANDS
WORREILU is a district within the ETHIOPIAN HIGHLANDS
ETHIOPIAN HIGHLANDS are located within the EAST AFRICAN HIGHLANDS
LANDSCAPE POSITIONS affect CROP-NUTRIENT RESPONSES
LANDSCAPE POSITIONS affect RESOURCES USE EFFICIENCY
UNDULATING LANDSCAPES are characterized by various LANDSCAPE POSITIONS
CATENA is used to understand SOIL VARIABILITY
SOIL VARIABILITY has implications for PRODUCTIVITY
